feedback.text: Hello, another fellow game enthusiast from Norway here. I really would like this to also include games as the way companies now just revoke our access to games we have purchased is not fair. You can also see how companies greed affect this with increasing costs of games, and the way they re-release games at a premium cost. Look at many of the top AAA or AAAA games like Assassins Creed or Forza Horizon for example where they simply just re-skin the game and change the map. There is often no new content (yes story changes, but its often bland) and they charge as much as they can, because they know their "fan-base" will buy it no matter what. This for me is greed. Now look at people that love old games because they own what they buy, now we only "own" what we pay for till the company decides the game dies and we loose access to what we love. It would be the same as starting your PC and suddenly you cant use it because a company stops supporting your PC, and the only way do use a PC again would be to buy a new one even when the hardware is good and functioning. With this analogy look at what the Windows 11 CPU requirements did. It made working hardware go to waste or be trashed in a landfill (Yes Linux exists but not all is comfortable with that). Now I'm on a tangent, but back to what's important here the games. I remember having a good time playing racing games my whole life and I got into The Crew. Mind I am mostly a single-player "Gamer", and usually didn't play with anyone on The Crew, but that got taken away from me without a though. I bought the game and all DLC's which was quite expensive and now I got nothing to show for it. It could be made to be played without the online features, albeit more limited, but we didn't get a choice. But its no problem for me to start up "Need for Speed Most Wanted (2005)" on my PS2 and play away on a 20 year old game. With how the prices now are and how the industry treats us as consumers this is not the way I see this moving forward. There needs to be more thought into how the games can be preserved forward. I would love someday to show my kids the games I played as a kid and for a while that was possible with the physical copies, but now this choice is taken away from us. The industry is moving more and more towards "Online-Only" games which require you to connect to a server which hosts much content and this makes it impossible to run if the server shuts down. If we could have access to the files for setting up and hosting servers ourself afterwards as many want this would allow for us to play the games again. Yes many have reverse-engineered many games to keep the memory alive, but this is not always possible or companies makes this extremely hard. Yes there is a reason for doing this when the service is online, but when its down, give us a way to do this ourself. Yes some companies says this is not feasible, because there will not be as much of moderators or as much security. This would then fall upon they who wish to host this and not the company as they would then not support this. Also most games that are kept alive is because of the joy it gave people who loved the game so much and wishes for it to be kept alive. If more from the Stop Killing Games initiative could be implemented that would mean more protections for us as consumers and the right to keep games alive. Thank you for listening / reading.
